questions.stem: Your IT team must enforce company coding standards and a compliance reminder on every Claude Code session on a machine, in every repository, in a way individual developers cannot exclude. Which mechanism fits?

options.text: A project `./CLAUDE.md` committed to every repository the team uses.
options.explanation: A project CLAUDE.md is per-repo and can be excluded with claudeMdExcludes; it isn't a machine-wide enforced policy.

options.text: A managed policy CLAUDE.md at the managed location, deployed via MDM.
options.explanation: A managed policy CLAUDE.md applies to every session in every repo on the machine, loads before user and project files, and cannot be excluded by individual settings.

options.text: A `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` pushed to each developer's home directory.
options.explanation: User-level files are personal and editable/removable by the developer, so they don't enforce organization-wide policy.

options.text: A `CLAUDE.local.md` committed at each repository's root.
options.explanation: CLAUDE.local.md is meant to be gitignored personal config and is neither machine-wide nor unexcludable.

questions.stem: Halfway through a long session you run `/compact` and worry your project standards will be lost. Which statement about what survives compaction is accurate?

options.text: Everything in the session is wiped, so re-paste your standards.
options.explanation: Compaction summarizes rather than wiping everything, and the project-root CLAUDE.md is re-injected automatically.

options.text: The project-root CLAUDE.md is re-read from disk and re-injected after compaction.
options.explanation: Project-root CLAUDE.md survives compaction because Claude re-reads it from disk and re-injects it; conversation-only instructions and not-yet-reloaded nested files may be lost.

options.text: Every nested subdirectory CLAUDE.md file in the whole project is always re-read and re-injected immediately too.
options.explanation: Nested CLAUDE.md files are not re-injected automatically; they reload only the next time Claude reads a file in that subdirectory.

options.text: Instructions given only in chat persist automatically through compaction.
options.explanation: Conversation-only instructions can be lost after compaction; that's why durable rules belong in CLAUDE.md.

questions.stem: You keep re-explaining the same build command and a debugging insight every session. You'd rather Claude record such learnings itself across sessions without you writing CLAUDE.md entries. Which mechanism does this?

options.text: Auto memory, where Claude saves learnings to its memory directory.
options.explanation: Auto memory lets Claude write notes like build commands and debugging insights to its per-repo memory directory and reload them in future sessions, no manual writing required.

options.text: Path-scoped rules, which record learnings as you edit files.
options.explanation: Rules are instructions you author, not notes Claude writes for itself across sessions.

options.text: The `/compact` command, which persists learnings to disk.
options.explanation: `/compact` summarizes the current conversation in-session; it isn't a cross-session learnings store.

options.text: A custom slash command that snapshots the conversation each turn.
options.explanation: A slash command is an on-demand action and doesn't automatically accumulate learnings across sessions the way auto memory does.

questions.stem: You invoked a coding-standards skill early in a long session, but after several other skills and a compaction, Claude seems to have stopped following it. What's the most reliable way to restore its full guidance?

options.text: Edit the skill file mid-session; the running session reloads it automatically each turn.
options.explanation: Claude Code does not re-read an invoked skill's file on later turns; editing it doesn't re-inject the already-loaded content.

options.text: Re-invoke the skill so its full content is re-attached to the context.
options.explanation: After compaction, re-attached skills share a limited budget and older ones can be dropped; re-invoking the skill restores its full content into context.

options.text: Run `/clear` to reset the session and lose the noise.
options.explanation: `/clear` discards the conversation context entirely, including the valuable work, rather than restoring the skill.

options.text: Lower the effort level so Claude focuses on the standards again.
options.explanation: Effort level tunes reasoning depth and doesn't restore dropped skill content to the context.
